paragraphs.text:
Brand identity, editorial systems & visual direction.
      Print, typography & risograph publications for cultural institutions.

A complete visual identity built around hand-drawn ligatures and a coral-on-charcoal palette, applied across posters, programmes and signage. The system flexes between three stage typologies while retaining a single, recognisable voice.

A 168-page bilingual catalogue accompanying the museum's seasonal programme, set in a custom Georgia revival and printed on uncoated stock. Generous margins and quiet pacing place the artwork firmly at the centre.

An updated wordmark and stationery suite for an independent Bergen publisher of children's literature. The new mark draws on hand-lettered school primers while sitting comfortably alongside contemporary trade titles.
A run of eight two-colour risograph posters announcing the 2022–23 stage season. Each composition reinterprets a single stage prop as a typographic anchor, printed in editions of 250.

A pilot signage and wayfinding study commissioned to test legibility at distance across mixed lighting conditions. The proposal pairs a stripped-back sans with directional pictograms in a single accent colour.

A modular identity for an ongoing residency series, built around a flexible grid that hosts each visiting artist's chosen colour and mark. The system runs across invitations, web headers and printed essays.
